title: Abstalar Zantus
summary: Sandpoint's high priest — a cleric of Desna who ministers to all faiths with steady, unhurried warmth.
content: When the Sandpoint chapel burnt to the ground in 4702 AR, killing the town's priest, Ezakien Tobyn, his position in the town was filled by his top acolyte, Abstalar Zantus. Though Father Zantus is himself a cleric of Desna, his role as high priest of the since-rebuilt Sandpoint Cathedral requires him to be open to other faiths, and he is more than willing and open to assist followers of other gods with spiritual matters.

title: Titus Scarnetti
summary: Head of the Scarnetti family — a man who has rebuilt his fortune through means he'd prefer to keep quiet, and who hasn't forgiven Sandpoint for not being afraid of him.
content:
Titus is the last significant patriarch of the Scarnetti line, operating Sandpoint's lumber industry and nursing grievances against the mayor, the Mercantile League, and the town's general failure to accord him the deference he considers his birthright.

Scarnetti is quite conservative and traditional in his thinking, which often puts him at odds with the other people in town. This disconnect has cost him the race for mayor every time he has run against the more likeable Kendra Deverin, and there is no love lost between the two.

Lately, there have been rumors that Titus has links to certain nefarious individuals in the Sandpoint area—including members of the Sczarni criminal gangs—which have greatly reduced his standing in town.3 The exits of fellow local nobles Ameiko Kaijitsu and the Valdemars have further eroded his influence, leading him to form the Scarnetti Consortium in an attempt to consolidate his power.

title: Ameiko Kaijitsu
summary: The former proprietor of the Rusty Dragon who left Sandpoint behind to reclaim her family's imperial birthright — she is now the Empress of Minkai.
content:
Born Ameiko Kaijitsu, her true surname is Amatatsu — the last surviving rightful heir to the throne of Minkai. Before her ascension, she was best known in Sandpoint as the proprietor of the Rusty Dragon, the most popular inn and tavern in town, and as a former adventurer.

In 4707 AR, Ameiko lost both her father and half-brother during the goblin raids tied to the rise of Runelord Karzoug. In 4711 AR, she and a group of adventurers discovered her true heritage and embarked on a journey across the Crown of the World to Minkai, where she defeated the usurper Jade Regent and claimed the Jade Throne.

As of 4724 AR, Amatatsu Ameiko is Empress of Minkai. She still returns to Sandpoint on occasion for visits.

title: Akoni
summary: A gentle male dryad who rules the fey court of Akoni's Grove in the Tickwood.
content: Akoni is a male dryad who makes his home in the Tickwood near Sandpoint, presiding over Akoni's Grove as lord of its fey court. His court is composed mainly of sprites and grigs, and he has long maintained a peaceful relationship with Sandpoint's hunters and nature-minded residents.

title: Runelord Sorshen
summary: The ancient Runelord of Lust — unique among her peers for having chosen reform over conquest, and instrumental in opposing the return of more destructive runelords.
content:
Runelord Sorshen was the Runelord of Lust in Ancient Thassilon, ruler of the realm of Eurythnia. Her ancient capital was Xin-Eurythnia, now the site of the modern city of Korvosa. Like her peers, she fled into magical stasis when Earthfall threatened the empire.

In recent years, Sorshen awakened and took a strikingly different path from the other returning Runelords — choosing to work against destructive forces rather than seek conquest. She was instrumental in empowering adventurers in their fight against the returning Runelord Alaznist, and invented the Runewaking Ritual, a crucial piece of magic for those hoping to permanently destroy Runelord Zutha.

Sorshen has been collecting the weapons of her fallen fellow Runelords, storing them in her personal demiplane, the Eye of Desire, to protect the world from their power. She now rules a portion of New Thassilon.

title: Elder Ordwi
summary: One of the Elders of Osprey Cove — a cleric of Cayden Cailean, a friend of the natural world, and a warm ally of the party.
content: Elder Ordwi is one of the governing council of Elders that leads Osprey Cove. She is a cleric of Cayden Cailean and a known friend of the natural world, embodying the town's values of freedom, community, and easy-going goodwill. She has become a personal friend of the party, and offered them hospitality — including lodging in the bar she runs — when they arrived in Osprey Cove.

title: Meitremar
summary: A scholar and operative of New Thassilon who is using research found in Rusthenge to attempt the resurrection of the dead demon lord Xar-Azmak.
content:
Meitremar is a dangerous scholar who is picking up where his grandfather left off: attempting the resurrection of Xar-Azmak, the long-dead demon lord of rust who once ruled the Abyssal realm of Vlorus. He has found the research necessary for this project within the Thassilonian ruin of Rusthenge near Iron Harbor.

Meitremar is positioned as a member of Runelord Belimarius's government in Xin-Edasseril, giving his work the political backing and resources of New Thassilon. In practice this relationship is one of convenience — his sinspawn ally Azomi, while nominally loyal to Belimarius, is loyal only to itself.

Meitremar's cult infiltrated and took over Stonehome in Iron Harbor, subverting the local Gorum worshippers, before they were soundly defeated. Meitremar's soul has been taken by the leigons of hell.

title: Runelord Belimarius
summary: The Runelord of Envy and ruler of half of New Thassilon — an ancient archmage who has reawakened from stasis and resumed command of her long-frozen capital Xin-Edasseril.
content:
Belimarius was the tenth and final Runelord of Envy, ruler of the Ancient Thassilonian realm of Edasseril. She reigned from -5402 AR until the fall of Thassilon in -5293 AR, when she and the inhabitants of her capital Xin-Edasseril were preserved outside of normal time rather than destroyed by Earthfall.

Very recently, adventurers freed Belimarius and the inhabitants of Xin-Edasseril from this state of captivity, and she has taken up the reins of leadership once again — now as co-ruler of the nation of New Thassilon alongside Runelord Sorshen. She wields Belimarius's Invidious Halberd, a weapon crafted by Xin himself for the previous Runelord of Envy.

The Runelord of Envy specializes in abjuration magic.

title: Runelord Xanderghul
summary: The one and only Runelord of Pride — a master of illusion who secretly also posed as the god known as the Peacock Spirit, worshipped by rune magic scholars throughout Thassilon.
content:
Runelord Xanderghul was the sole holder of the title of Runelord of Pride throughout all of Ancient Thassilon's history — there was only ever one. As the Runelord of Pride, he specialized in illusion magic, whose rune is the Crescent Scepter, and his sin's energy is violet.

Whether Xanderghul survived Earthfall in magical stasis, as some of his peers did, remains unknown.

Perhaps he was too prideful to run.

title: Vanda
summary: A former Stonehome acolyte who has not yet fully surrendered to the cult of Xar-Azmak — still wearing her unrusted plate, still carrying a thread of remorse.
content:
Vanda was one of the acolytes of Stonehome, the garrison-church of Gorum in Iron Harbor, who fell under the influence of Meitremar's cult of Xar-Azmak when the Iron Harbor was infiltrated. Unlike the others who fully converted — trading their unrusted steel for the rusted armor and sickles that mark devotion to the dead demon lord — Vanda never made that final surrender.

In the wake of the crisis, Vanda stepped up to lead Stonehome, taking on the responsibilities of a temple left without clear direction after the corruption was purged. She has since thrown herself into rebuilding both the church and the community's trust in it.

She is currently in a situationship with Ashka B'gosh.

title: Naffer Vosk
summary: The caretaker of Sandpoint's boneyard — a former smuggler who found redemption in Sarenrae, and tends the dead with fierce devotion.
content: Naffer walks with an awkward lurch from a twisted spine he's had since birth, but he maintains the Sandpoint Boneyard in meticulous condition and rings the cathedral bells at dawn, noon, and dusk without fail.

title: Belor Hemlock
summary: Sandpoint's Shoanti sheriff — he took the job on the worst night of the town's history and never gave it back.
content: Belor Hemlock changed his name from Viskalai — the Shoanti word for hemlock — as a gesture of belonging to the town that elected him in the aftermath of Chopper's rampage. His brother has never forgiven him for it.

title: Kendra Deverin
summary: Sandpoint's mayor — fiery-haired, sharp-minded, and genuinely liked by the people she governs, which her rivals find deeply inconvenient.
content: Kendra didn't want to be mayor. She won anyway, and has been better at the job than most expected. She governs with a personal touch that the founding families find insufficiently aristocratic and the townspeople find refreshing.

title: Cyrdak Drokkus
summary: Sandpoint's showman — theatrical, charming, and in possession of a past in Magnimar he'll reference constantly but never actually explain.
content: Cyrdak runs the Sandpoint Theater with a flamboyance that has made him both beloved and indispensable. He's married to the paladin Jasper Korvaski, which he considers proof that Sandpoint was always where he was supposed to be.

title: Jasper Korvaski
summary: Sandpoint's town planner and retired paladin of Abadar — incorruptible in ways that make him both invaluable and occasionally inconvenient.
content: Jasper handles Sandpoint's planning, zoning, and property disputes with the same nigh-incorruptible character that once made him a paladin. The Scarnettis have tried to push him out of town. He has declined to leave.

title: Bethana Corwin
summary: The Rusty Dragon's quiet halfling manager — far more comfortable with linens and ledgers than with crowds.
content: Bethana is introverted to the point of near-invisibility in a busy tavern, which suits her fine. She handles the maid services and room rentals and leaves the socializing to everyone else.

title: Daviren Hosk
summary: Security and muscle at the Rusty Dragon — and the man in Sandpoint with the most personal reasons to hate goblins.
content: Daviren lost an eye to a goblin horsechopper during the Thistletop raid. He added eight goblin ears to his collection in the same fight. His feelings on the subject have not moderated since.

title: Garridan Viskalai
summary: Owner of the White Deer — a Shoanti man who stayed in Sandpoint out of love for his family and has never quite forgiven himself for it.
content: Garridan runs the White Deer with quiet efficiency. He regrets that his parents gave up their Shoanti roots to settle in Sandpoint, and resents that his brother did the same thing — and then renamed himself after it.

title: Jargie Quinn
summary: The one-legged, endlessly gregarious owner of the Hagfish — a champion liar in the best possible sense.
content: Jargie Quinn is the kind of man who can hold a room just by opening his mouth. His hagfish Norah has made the Hagfish famous. How he lost his leg is a different story every time and somehow always entertaining.

title: Jesk 'Cracktooth' Berinni
summary: Owner of Cracktooth's Tavern — looks like a thug, thinks like a satirist, and is funnier than anyone expects.
content: Jesk is well read, sharp-witted, and slightly dangerous. His amateur political commentary on Magnimar's ruling class has made him a minor local celebrity. Do not judge him by his teeth.

title: Risa Magravi
summary: Sandpoint's oldest innkeeper — a half-blind Varisian sorcerer whose tales of strange places are actually true, and who is quietly dying because she can no longer dream.
content: Risa built her tavern and her legend in equal measure. She travels the Dreamlands every night in her sleep — or she used to. Something is blocking her dreams now, and without them she believes she is fading.

title: Kaye Tresarani
summary: Proprietor of the Pixie's Kitten — well-connected, quietly formidable, and in a long-running relationship with the sheriff that neither of them advertises.
content: Kaye runs her establishment with a shrewdness that extends well beyond the front door. She has a seat on the town council, a relationship with Belor Hemlock that the town politely pretends not to notice, and ears in places most people forget are listening.

title: Vorvashali Voon
summary: The gregarious proprietor of the Feathered Serpent — a collector and dealer in magical oddities who is currently very interested in clockwork birds.
content: Vorvashali is enthusiastic about almost everything and especially enthusiastic about magical curiosities. Nine out of ten adventurers will tell you one of Vorvashali's items saved their hid, and the tenth didn't make it out of the dungeon.

title: Chask Haladan
summary: Owner of the Curious Goblin — a retired adventurer who has spent seventy years loving books and shows no sign of stopping.
content: Chask is old, unhurried, and surrounded by books he genuinely doesn't want to sell. He'll talk about almost any of them at length if you let him. He's kept the shop financially afloat for decades on the strength of a lucky adventuring career and a very frugal lifestyle.

title: Savah Bevaniky
summary: Sandpoint's arms dealer — practical, professional, and quietly worried about why her town needs so many adventurers.
content: Savah runs a clean operation and keeps good stock. She's noticed that every time someone cleans her out of heavy crossbows, something terrible is about to happen to Sandpoint. She is currently well-stocked.

title: Das Korvut
summary: Sandpoint's smith — brilliant at his work and legendarily unpleasant about everything else.
content: Das Korvut is bald, powerfully built, and constitutionally incapable of suffering fools. He produces exceptional metalwork. He will make you feel terrible about asking for it. The local children have a rhyme about him.

title: Aliver 'Pillbug' Podiker
summary: Sandpoint's alchemist — a poison dealer if you know how to ask hiding behind a respectable storefront.
content: An unassuming, if strange, alchemist. Just as likely to offer a healing poltice as a deadly poison, depending on his judgement of your needs.

title: Ilsoari Gandethus
summary: Headmaster of Turandarok Academy — a retired adventurer who runs an orphanage and keeps a locked museum of the things he killed, with one notable regret.
content: Ilsoari is the kind of retired adventurer who chose to do something useful with his time. He runs the school and orphanage with genuine care. His basement collection is extraordinary. And there's a dungeon he never got to finish exploring — the Pit — that he's never quite gotten over.

title: Ibor Thorn
summary: Manager of the Sandpoint Lumber Mill — a man whose nightmares about the machinery are getting harder to dismiss as stress.
content: Ibor keeps the mill running and himself together through sheer stubbornness. His nightmares about the logsplitter have crossed a line from concerning to alarming, and people close to him are starting to notice.

title: Hannah Velerin
summary: Sandpoint's herbalist and healer — a town council member quietly dealing with a threatening situation she hasn't taken to the authorities yet.
content: Hannah is trusted by most of Sandpoint and sits on the town council. She handles the anonymous threats showing up at her door with the patience of someone who has dealt with worse, and the caution of someone who knows that patience has limits.

title: Brodert Quink
summary: Sandpoint's Thassilonian scholar — decades of study and no prestige to show for it, until recently when people started realizing he'd been right all along.
content: Brodert studied with dwarven engineers at Janderhoff and catalogued ruins at the Founder's Archive in Magnimar. He's spent years insisting the Old Light was a war machine. The town is only now catching up to him, and he has mixed feelings about being vindicated this way.

title: Gorvi
summary: Sandpoint's dung-sweeper — one of the town's few half-orcs, paid well enough to live better than he does, and seemingly content with his shack and his crew.
content: Gorvi employs over two dozen youths to help manage Sandpoint's waste. He lives in a small, run-down shack despite earning a comfortable wage, and nobody has yet worked out whether that's philosophy or stubbornness.

title: Ven Vinder
summary: Owner of Sandpoint's general store — a father who lost a daughter to a serial killer and has been losing himself to grief ever since.
content: Ven used to be one of those men who measured their worth in their family. Now he measures it in how many hours he can spend in his cellar drinking before anyone comes looking for him.

title: Solsta Vinder
summary: Ven Vinder's wife — keeping the general store running, keeping the family together, and quietly terrified that she's losing both her husband and her daughter.
content: Solsta manages the store, manages her husband's deterioration, and is starting to manage her fear about where Shayliss keeps disappearing to.

title: Shayliss Vinder
summary: The Vinders' surviving, yet prodigal, daughter
content: Shayliss has become someone her parents don't quite recognize. She's found faith in Calistria seemingly out of nowhere. Whatever she's doing when she slips out of the secret door by the Turandarok River, it isn't what her mother imagines.

title: Larz Rovanky
summary: Owner of the Rovanky Tannery — a perfectionist who produces excellent leather goods, keeps firing his employees, and sees nothing contradictory about this.
content: Larz expects perfection. He has never met an employee who meets that standard for long. The leather goods are worth the wait. The wait is sometimes very long.

title: Hayliss Korvaski
summary: Jasper's sister and fellow Abadar devotee — without her brother's restraint, and not at all sorry about it.
content: Hayliss runs her shop with the same faith her brother Jasper holds, and none of his diplomatic temperament. She charges Scarnetti family members extra and considers the mayor's requests to keep the peace to be advisory at best. She has recently seen something unusual involving the Vinder girl, and she's looking for someone discreet to investigate.
